reasons: Hayabusa can punish Vale when Vale is isolated or after Vale uses Windblow.
Shadow mobility makes Vale's aimed combo harder to land cleanly.

proof.summary: Vale depends on landing skillshot control and burst timing, while Hayabusa can wait for the key control spell and burst Vale during cooldown.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa attacks after Vale uses Windblow.
Vale is isolated from teammates or minions.
failureCases: Vale's team peels Hayabusa before he finishes.

reasons: Harley can blink in with Space Escape, apply burst, and return before Vale fully answers.
Vale is vulnerable when Harley attacks from fog or after Vale uses Windblow.

proof.summary: Vale wants controlled spacing to land wind combo, while Harley can quickly enter with Space Escape, burst with Deadly Magic and Poker Trick, and retreat to his hat.
worksBestWhen: Harley initiates from bush or fog.
Harley waits until Vale's control skill is unavailable.
failureCases: Vale holds Windblow and catches Harley's entry.
Harley's burst is blocked or absorbed by a tanky teammate.
Harley fails to burst Vale before returning.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge or bypass Vale's area timing.
Vale is punishable if Lancelot reaches him after his control spell is used.

proof.summary: Vale wants to catch enemies with wind control, while Lancelot can use mobility and invulnerability windows to bypass the setup and punish cooldowns.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Vale to use Windblow.
Lancelot has minions or nearby targets for dash extension.
failureCases: Vale saves crowd control for Lancelot.
Vale positions too far behind frontline.

reasons: Natalia can still punish Vale from hidden angles.
The matchup is conditional because Vale can burst or control Natalia if his full combo is ready.

proof.summary: Vale wants safe space to aim his combo, while Natalia pressures him from hidden angles; this works best when Vale's control skill is unavailable or he is isolated.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks from side bush or fog.

reasons: Chou can use Shunpo timing to approach and kick Vale out of position.
This gives a fighter-style answer when the team needs pickoff instead of pure assassin burst.

proof.summary: Vale wants to cast from safe distance, while Chou can time immunity, close distance, and displace Vale into allied follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Chou saves Shunpo for Vale's crowd-control timing.
failureCases: Chou kicks Vale away from allied damage.
Chou cannot reach Vale through peel.
